authoritative_sources.title: Certified Community Behavioral Health Clinic Certification Criteria
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's CCBHC criteria are a primary named-industry baseline for behavioral health and community care programs. They define required evaluations, treatment planning cadence, care coordination, and health information system expectations that drive documentation workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: program-model classification and treatment-plan documentation grounding
used_for: systems-of-record assumptions for health information systems

authoritative_sources.title: Community Mental Health Centers
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS publishes the Conditions of Participation for CMHCs, a primary federal operating baseline for community mental health documentation and health and safety requirements in Medicare-participating settings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: community mental health record obligations and compliance boundary setting

authoritative_sources.title: State Operations Manual Appendix F - Community Mental Health Centers Interpretive Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS Appendix F is the public interpretive workflow reference for CMHC documentation, record review, treatment plans, discharge records, and release controls. It is directly tied to the lane's administrative record-maintenance tasks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record validation, maintenance, and discharge or release packaging
used_for: required record contents and review posture
adjacent privacy and clinician handoff triggers

authoritative_sources.title: Documentation Matters Toolkit: Behavioral Health Practitioners
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS's Documentation Matters fact sheet is a recognized public workflow reference for behavioral-health note quality, signatures, dates, amendments, and evidentiary sufficiency in documentation review.
used_for: failure-mode design for cloned, incomplete, or unsupported notes

authoritative_sources.title: Individuals' Right under HIPAA to Access their Health Information
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR's access guidance is authoritative for designated-record-set scope, timeliness, and access-denial boundaries, all of which directly constrain behavioral-health documentation release work.

authoritative_sources.title: Does HIPAA provide extra protections for mental health information compared with other health information?
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR's FAQ is authoritative for the distinction between psychotherapy notes and the rest of the medical record and therefore directly constrains what a behavioral documentation lane may classify, package, and release.

authoritative_sources.title: Understanding Confidentiality of Substance Use Disorder (SUD) Patient Records or "Part 2"
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR's current Part 2 guidance is authoritative for SUD record confidentiality, consent posture, redisclosure handling, and legal-proceeding limits after the recent Part 2 and HIPAA alignment changes. Those rules materially change behavioral-health documentation workflow when SUD records are present.
used_for: SUD consent, counseling note, and legal-proceeding artifact controls

workflow_stages.name: Program-model and request classification
workflow_stages.description: Identify the care setting, jurisdiction, request type, document purpose, and whether the record set includes specially protected behavioral-health or SUD materials before work is accepted as in scope.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Behavioral-health record validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate intake, assessment, treatment-plan, note, screening, discharge, and ROI artifacts for completeness, signatures, version alignment, and correct classification across systems.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Administrative maintenance and packet assembly
workflow_stages.description: Prepare document completeness summaries, update notes, disclosure packets, utilization-review support packets, and audit-ready exception notes without deciding the underlying clinical or legal question.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Retention, access, and governance escalation
workflow_stages.description: Resolve or escalate designated-record-set, psychotherapy-note, Part 2, correction, retention, and release conflicts by preserving evidence and routing to the right owner rather than improvising.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: treatment plans and periodic review records
progress notes, service notes, and co-signature records
measurement-based care or screening tools such as PHQ-9, GAD-7, or C-SSRS when present
crisis-plan, safety-plan, and incident or event documentation references
release-of-information, consent, and designated-record-set request records
psychotherapy-note classification markers and separate-storage controls when applicable
Part 2 consent and redisclosure artifacts when applicable
discharge summaries, continuity-of-care packets, and utilization-review support records
amendment or correction requests, disclosure logs, and audit-trail bundles
systems_of_record: behavioral health EHR or clinical documentation platform
document repository or health information management system
release-of-information or privacy request workflow system
payer or utilization-review portal when documentation is assembled for review
audit logging and retention control systems

allowed: Classify behavioral-health documentation work as in scope, out of scope, blocked, or requiring escalation based on documented records and authority context.
Validate completeness, currency, signatures, classification, and source traceability of behavioral-health records and evidence bundles.
Prepare administrative summaries, update notes, evidence packets, and governance escalations without overstating authority.
Apply stricter tenant controls when they narrow but do not contradict the public baseline.
escalate: Program model, jurisdiction, designated-record-set definition, psychotherapy-note classification, or Part 2 applicability is missing and would change the answer.
Retention, release, correction, or source-precedence rules conflict across public guidance, tenant policy, or payer requirements.
The request requires clinician, privacy, legal, utilization-review, or patient-safety authority.
Records conflict across the EHR, document repository, or release workflow and the source-of-truth owner has not reconciled them.
refuse: Requests to backdate, rewrite, remove, or sanitize official behavioral-health records.
Requests to release psychotherapy notes, Part 2 material, or other specially protected information without the required authorization or owner approval.
Requests to approve clinical, legal, payer, or official record outcomes outside administrative documentation scope.
Requests that treat behavioral-health documentation as generic admin work and bypass the required note, consent, safety, or disclosure controls.

domain_failure_modes: Treating behavioral-health charts like generic document packets and omitting treatment-plan, screening, safety-plan, or ROI artifacts.
Conflating psychotherapy notes with the designated record set and releasing or withholding the wrong material.
Ignoring Part 2 applicability when SUD records are present inside the behavioral-health episode.
Marking documentation complete while co-signatures, treatment-plan reviews, or discharge records remain missing.
Letting the lane rewrite clinical content or decide medical necessity because record quality and clinical ownership were not separated clearly.
Certifying conflicting note versions or encounter dates across EHR, quality, and payer-facing systems.
Assuming a tenant's behavioral-health program follows CCBHC or CMHC documentation rules without verifying the actual program model.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own
Low-confidence escalation when required facts or authority are missing

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific retention, amendment, and minor-consent rules
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Behavioral-health record retention windows, amendment rights, and who can authorize disclosure for minors or guardians vary materially by state and program.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant retrieval of jurisdiction-specific policy and escalate when state law or tenant policy changes the outcome.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Program-model variance across outpatient, crisis, CMHC, CCBHC, and non-CMS behavioral-health settings
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different program models can require different treatment-plan review cadence, document sets, and quality reporting posture.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat program model as a required tenant fact before claiming completeness against a specific public baseline.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant storage model for psychotherapy notes and Part 2 segments
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Some tenants segregate these materials in separate repositories while others use classification markers inside the same platform, which changes retrieval and release handling.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant source-of-truth and classification mapping before preparing release-ready packets.
